task_completion.description: A search input component is integrated into the main activity layout.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Android Studio editor, a layout file (e.g., activity_main.xml) or Compose UI file is open, showing the addition of a search input component such as SearchView, EditText, or TextField.

task_completion.description: Logic is implemented to filter plants by name, description, or grow zone number.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Kotlin code is visible showing a filtering operation on the plant list that compares the search query against the 'name', 'description', and 'growZoneNumber' properties of the Plant objects.

task_completion.description: The search feature shows all plants when the query is empty.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Kotlin code is visible showing a condition that checks if the search query is empty or blank, and if so, returns or displays the full, unfiltered list of plants.

task_completion.description: The displayed list updates in real-time as the user types.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Kotlin code is visible showing a text change listener (e.g., setOnQueryTextListener, addTextChangedListener, or Compose onValueChange) that triggers the filtering logic and updates the UI list dynamically.

task_completion.description: The project compiles successfully after all changes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Android Studio 'Build' tool window shows a successful build message (e.g., 'BUILD SUCCESSFUL' or 'Build: completed successfully') with no compilation errors.

integrity.description: The agent used the Android Studio IDE to edit files and build the project.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent actively using the Android Studio interface to write code and trigger builds, rather than solely relying on terminal commands like 'echo' or 'sed'.

integrity.description: The filtering logic dynamically evaluates plant properties rather than hardcoding expected results.
integrity.visual_evidence: The code uses standard string matching and filtering functions (e.g., contains, filter) rather than returning a hardcoded list of specific plants for a specific test query.

integrity.description: The build success is genuine and executed within the IDE.
integrity.visual_evidence: The build success message appears in the actual Android Studio Build/Run panel with corresponding task execution logs, not simulated in a text file or terminal.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The app uses a hardcoded dataset of 5 plants in PlantRepository.kt: Apple (zone 3), Beet (zone 2), Cilantro (zone 2), Tomato (zone 9), and Sunflower (zone 8). The Plant data class contains properties: plantId, name, description, growZoneNumber, wateringInterval, and imageUrl. The filtering logic must check the 'name', 'description', and 'growZoneNumber' properties. The project is located at /home/ga/AndroidStudioProjects/SunflowerApp and uses package com.google.samples.apps.sunflower.
